violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH CFP at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC relocated can opener to ware washing area to be washed, rinsed and sanitized at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. All equipment/utensils to be sanitized at the 3 Bay Sink after washing in the dish machine until unit is serviced/repaired. Call was made for service at the time of inspection. Technician to arrive same day for service. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Garlic Butter (TCS food) was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Garlic Butter was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. (Garlic Butter was double panned in the cold hold unit at the time of inspection. Per the PIC, Garlic Butter will no longer be double panned.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Working containers of food ingredients were observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Foods labeled at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

The blades to the Food Processor and Deli Slicers in the back prep area were observed to be bent/chipped/gouged and not in good repair. Blades are to be serviced/repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Internal areas of cold drawer units - Surfaces between equipment along the cookline/prep area Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2024
